seoTitle: How to Extract Audio From a Video in Canva
metaDescription: Learn how to separate and extract audio from a video in Canva so you can reuse the sound in other projects.
intro: Sometimes you just need the audio track from a video, not the whole clip. This tutorial shows how to extract audio from a video in Canva, so you can reuse sound bites for podcasts, presentations, or other content.

steps.heading: Access the audio feature
steps.body: Open your video project in Canva and locate the tool for handling audio tracks.

steps.heading: Extract the audio
steps.body: Separate the audio track from the video clip so it can be used independently.

steps.heading: Use the extracted audio
steps.body: Apply the extracted audio to other projects, such as podcasts or presentations that need that specific sound.

steps.heading: Follow best practices
steps.body: Keep audio quality in mind when extracting, checking the result before relying on it in a new project.

faq.question: Will extracting audio reduce the quality of the sound?
faq.answer: Generally the audio quality should stay close to the source, though it depends on the original video's audio quality.

faq.question: Can I extract audio from any video uploaded to Canva?
faq.answer: In most cases yes, as long as the video file includes an audio track and you have rights to use it.

faq.question: What can I do with the extracted audio afterward?
faq.answer: You can reuse it in other Canva projects, download it, or repurpose it for things like podcasts or voiceovers.

faq.question: Is this feature available on Canva's free plan?
faq.answer: Availability can depend on your specific Canva plan, so it's worth checking your account's feature access.
